name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Rochester, Ny
description_short: NACA is a non-profit housing advocacy organization offering HUD-approved housing counseling and character-based mortgage lending to help close racial wealth gaps through affordable homeownership.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years working as a housing advocacy and services organization. Originally established to challenge predatory lending practices, NACA has evolved into a comprehensive non-profit focused on closing racial wealth disparity through accessible homeownership programs.

NACA offers a distinctive mortgage product called the "Best in America Mortgage" featuring no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration. They also provide HUD-approved housing counseling—handling 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally—and operate "Achieve the Dream" events in multiple cities where prospective members can become NACA-Qualified. The organization maintains a member portal for document uploads, file management, and status tracking, plus volunteer opportunities through their NACtivist program.

What distinguishes NACA is their character-based lending philosophy that explicitly does not consider credit scores, combined with their advocacy work against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors. They've helped over 500,000 homeowners and currently have 75,000 active NACA mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments. Their dual focus on both lending products and community activism around housing justice sets them apart from traditional mortgage lenders.

However, NACA functions primarily as a non-profit counseling and advocacy organization rather than a consumer finance company in the traditional sense. Access to their mortgage products requires attending multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events and becoming NACA-Qualified, which involves significant time commitment. While their mission is compelling and their track record substantial, they are better categorized as a free/non-profit housing counseling service than a direct mortgage lender comparable to commercial banks or mortgage companies.
pros: No credit score consideration—character-based lending focuses on circumstances rather than traditional credit metrics
Zero down payment and zero closing costs, eliminating major barriers to homeownership
Provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally, demonstrating significant non-profit counseling capacity
Fixed-rate mortgages below market (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026) without mortgage insurance requirements
Free membership and counseling services; no upfront fees for qualification or services
Volunteer opportunities (NACtivist program) allow members to participate in advocacy and community organizing
Has served 3 million people and helped 500,000+ become homeowners with $20 billion in mortgage commitments
cons: Requires attending multi-day in-person 'Achieve the Dream' events (Friday-Sunday) to become NACA-Qualified before accessing mortgage products
Limited geographic accessibility—events held only in select cities (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis) several times per year
Website lacks detailed information about specific income requirements, debt-to-income ratios, employment verification needs, or other underwriting criteria
No clear timeline stated for mortgage approval or closing after becoming NACA-Qualified
Primarily advocacy-focused organization; mortgage lending is one component rather than primary business function
best_for: First-time homebuyers with fair or poor credit histories who want to avoid predatory lending
Lower-income families and historically marginalized communities facing racial wealth gaps
Prospective homebuyers with limited down payment savings who live near or can travel to event cities
Community members interested in housing justice advocacy and volunteer engagement alongside homeownership
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers with limited credit history or poor credit who can commit time to attending regional events and want to work with a values-driven non-profit focused on housing justice. The main caveat is that this is a non-profit counseling and advocacy organization primarily, not a commercial mortgage lender, so accessing their mortgages requires multi-day event attendance and the organization's lending volume is limited compared to traditional mortgage companies.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of national HUD counseling volume)
Character-based mortgage lending with no credit score consideration
Best in America Mortgage product (no down payment, no closing costs, no mortgage insurance)
Achieve the Dream multi-day events for NACA qualification
Member portal for document uploads and file management
NACtivist volunteer program and activism opportunities
Advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors
Rental experience collection and documentation services
Community organizing and housing justice campaigns
Partner resources for lenders, servicers, investors, contractors, agents, inspectors
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
